series.title: Envoy AI Gateway v0.7.x
series.subtitle: Hostname-based routing, Anthropic-to-Bedrock Converse translation, multimodal audio/video content types, MCP authorization-aware tool listing, and SSE streaming fixes.

releases.overview: Envoy AI Gateway v0.7.0 adds hostname-based routing to AIGatewayRoute, enabling multi-tenant deployments where different hostnames expose different model sets through a single Gateway. A new Anthropic Messages → AWS Bedrock Converse translator lets Anthropic-native clients reach Bedrock without switching protocols. Multimodal support grows with audio_url and video_url content types for OpenAI-compatible backends. MCP tools/list responses now respect authorization rules so callers only see tools they're allowed to invoke. Anthropic backends gain prefix support for custom request paths, and several SSE streaming and provider translation bugs are fixed.

items.title: Hostname-based model scoping on AIGatewayRoute
items.description: Serve different model sets from a single Gateway by assigning hostnames to each AIGatewayRoute. The /v1/models endpoint automatically returns only the models declared by routes matching the request's Host header, so tenants on teamA.ai.example.com and teamB.ai.example.com each see their own catalog without separate Gateways. Wildcard hostnames (*.ai.example.com) are supported following the Gateway API hostname matching rules.

items.title: Anthropic /v1/messages → AWS Bedrock Converse API
items.description: Send requests in Anthropic Messages format and have them translated to Bedrock's Converse and ConverseStream APIs automatically. Supports text, images, tool use, thinking blocks, and streaming — so Anthropic-native clients can reach any Bedrock model without changing their integration. Complements the existing OpenAI → Bedrock Converse and Anthropic → Bedrock InvokeModel paths.

items.title: Custom request paths for Anthropic backends via prefix
items.description: The prefix field on VersionedAPISchema now works for Anthropic-schema backends, producing endpoints like /{prefix}/messages instead of the default /v1/messages. Useful for routing to Anthropic-compatible providers that use a non-standard path.

items.title: Anthropic anthropic-beta header forwarded to AWSAnthropic
items.description: The anthropic-beta request header is now mapped into the anthropic_beta body field when routing to AWSAnthropic backends, so beta features like extended thinking and token counting work through the gateway without manual body rewriting.

items.title: audio_url and video_url content types
items.description: OpenAI chat completion requests can now include audio_url and video_url content parts, enabling multimodal audio and video inputs for compatible backends like vLLM with phi-4-mm and Qwen 3.5 models.

items.title: Authorization-filtered tools/list responses
items.description: MCP tools/list now applies the same authorization rules used by tools/call, omitting tools the caller isn't authorized to invoke. Prevents unauthorized callers from discovering tool names and avoids wasted LLM turns on tools that would fail at call time.

apiChanges.title: AIGatewayRoute.spec.hostnames
apiChanges.description: New optional field accepting a list of hostnames for hostname-based request filtering. When specified, the generated HTTPRoute includes these hostnames, and the /v1/models endpoint scopes its response to models from matching routes. Follows Gateway API hostname semantics including wildcard support.

apiChanges.title: AIGatewayRoute.spec.rules capped at 15
apiChanges.description: Maximum rules per AIGatewayRoute reduced from 128 to 15 to match the Gateway API HTTPRoute limit (one slot is reserved for a controller-injected catch-all rule). To configure more rules on the same Gateway, split them across multiple AIGatewayRoute resources.

apiChanges.title: VersionedAPISchema.prefix supported for Anthropic
apiChanges.description: The prefix field now applies to Anthropic-schema backends in addition to OpenAI. The version field is ignored for Anthropic; use prefix for custom paths. Note: prefix is ignored for AWSAnthropic and GCPAnthropic as these override paths internally.

bugFixes.title: SSE parser handles fields without space after colon
bugFixes.description: The SSE event parser now correctly handles fields formatted as data:{json} (no space after the colon), in addition to the standard data: {json}. Fixes silent field drops when proxying responses from providers that omit the optional space.

bugFixes.title: Responses API streaming SSE buffering
bugFixes.description: OpenAI Responses API and speech streaming translators now buffer incomplete SSE events across response body chunks instead of treating each chunk as self-contained. Fixes dropped or mangled events when TCP segment boundaries split an SSE event mid-frame.

bugFixes.title: Nil output guard in AWS Bedrock response translator
bugFixes.description: Bedrock can return HTTP 200 with no output field (e.g. guardrail interventions or UnknownOperationException). Previously this caused a nil-pointer panic in the ext-proc; now it returns a clean error to the caller.

bugFixes.title: Comprehensive Gemini finish-reason mapping
bugFixes.description: Gemini finish reasons like SAFETY, BLOCKLIST, RECITATION, MALFORMED_FUNCTION_CALL, and others now map to their correct OpenAI equivalents instead of all falling through to content_filter. Unknown reasons map to error rather than silently misreporting as a content filter event.

bugFixes.title: Empty delta in GCP Vertex AI streaming chunks
bugFixes.description: Streaming response chunks from GCP Vertex AI that lack candidate content now emit an empty delta object instead of omitting the field, conforming to the OpenAI streaming contract and fixing parse errors in strict clients.

bugFixes.title: Typeless assistant output messages in Responses API
bugFixes.description: Multi-turn Responses API inputs that include assistant messages without an explicit type: "message" field (e.g. from OpenCode) now parse correctly. Previously these were treated as easy-input messages, causing unmarshalling failures on output_text content blocks.

dependencies.title: Go 1.26.2
dependencies.description: Continues on Go 1.26.2, same as v0.6.

dependencies.title: Envoy Gateway v1.7.0
dependencies.description: Built on Envoy Gateway v1.7.0 for proven data plane capabilities.

dependencies.title: Envoy v1.37
dependencies.description: Leveraging Envoy Proxy v1.37.0 for battle-tested networking.

dependencies.title: Gateway API v1.4.1
dependencies.description: Support for Gateway API v1.4.1 specifications.

dependencies.title: Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2
dependencies.description: Continued integration with Gateway API Inference Extension v1.0.2 for intelligent endpoint selection.
